question: True or False? If (b_n) coverges to b then |b_n| converges to |b|. Answer True or False.

conversation.content: True or False? If (b_n) coverges to b then |b_n| converges to |b|. Answer True or False.

True. If b_n → b then | |b_n| - |b| | ≤ |b_n - b| by the reverse triangle inequality; since the right side → 0, |b_n| → |b|.  
then the answer: True

True. By the reverse triangle inequality, ||b_n| - |b|| ≤ |b_n - b| for every n. Since b_n → b we have |b_n - b| → 0, hence ||b_n| - |b|| → 0, so |b_n| → |b|.

True. Proof: For any n, by the reverse triangle inequality ||b_n| - |b|| ≤ |b_n - b|. Given ε>0 choose N with |b_n - b| < ε for n ≥ N (since b_n → b). Then for n ≥ N we have ||b_n| - |b|| < ε, so |b_n| → |b|.  
then the answer: True
